data.title: Prosper Construction Formalizes Terms of Reference for Nomination Committee
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Prosper Construction Holdings Limited ( (HK:6816) ).

Prosper Construction Holdings has set out formal terms of reference for its nomination committee, detailing its establishment, composition requirements, and operating procedures. The committee must comprise at least three directors with a majority of independent non-executive members and at least one member of a different gender, and it will oversee director nomination processes in line with strengthened governance standards.

The document specifies rules on meeting notices, quorum, use of electronic meeting formats, and written resolutions, while confirming that members cannot appoint alternates and may seek information from employees and professional advisers. These measures formalize the committee’s authority and processes, reinforcing the group’s corporate governance framework and transparency for shareholders and other stakeholders.

More about Prosper Construction Holdings Limited

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited operates in the construction sector, providing building and related engineering services through its group subsidiaries. The company is governed by a board that includes executive and independent non-executive directors, supported by formal board committees to oversee corporate governance and regulatory compliance.

data.title: Prosper Construction Formalizes Audit Committee Mandate to Strengthen Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited ( (HK:6816) ) has provided an announcement.

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited has set out formal terms of reference for its Audit Committee, originally established by a board resolution on 22 June 2016, detailing how members are appointed, removed, and structured to align with Hong Kong Listing Rules. The document specifies that the committee must comprise at least three non-executive directors, with a majority being independent and at least one possessing professional accounting or financial expertise, while also barring recent partners of the current auditing firm and formalizing procedures for convening meetings, notices and secretarial support to strengthen corporate governance.

Under the updated framework, the Audit Committee chairman must be an independent non-executive director appointed by the board or elected by committee members, and the company secretary will serve as the committee’s secretary unless a substitute is chosen for a specific meeting. These measures are designed to reinforce the independence, transparency and effectiveness of Prosper Construction’s financial oversight and audit processes, which may enhance investor confidence and compliance with regulatory standards.

More about Prosper Construction Holdings Limited

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the construction sector. The group operates through a board comprising executive and non-executive directors, including independent non-executive directors, and is subject to the corporate governance and listing requirements of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

The latest announcement is out from Hands Form Holdings Limited ( (HK:1920) ).

China Wacan Group Company Limited has completed a connected transaction involving the subscription and issue of 112,320,000 new shares under a specific mandate, following the fulfillment of all conditions in the subscription agreement. Neusoft Education Technology Co. Limited ( (HK:9616) ) has issued an update.

Neutech Group Limited announced that its controlling shareholder’s subsidiaries, Dongkong First and Dongkong Second, have had all previously pledged shares in the company fully released and discharged as of 10 April 2026. These pledged shares, totaling 247,465,000 and once securing a RMB325.5 million loan, had represented about 38.30% of Neutech’s issued share capital at the time of the original pledge.

Following the release, Dongkong First and Dongkong Second now collectively hold approximately 39.29% of Neutech’s total issued shares, reaffirming the stability of the controlling shareholder’s position. The removal of the share pledge eases concerns over potential enforced disposals tied to loan covenants and may be viewed positively by investors monitoring the company’s ownership structure and governance risk profile.

data.title: Ab&B Bio-Tech Auditor Resigns Amid Probe Into Fund Investments and Consultancy Payments
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ab&B Bio-Tech CO., LTD. JS Class H ( (HK:2627) ) just unveiled an update.

Ab&B Bio-Tech announced that Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu has resigned as its auditor with immediate effect amid an ongoing delay in publishing the company’s 2025 annual results and a suspension of share trading in Hong Kong. Deloitte cited grave concerns over the commercial substance and business rationale of approximately HK$272 million in fund investments and about HK$58 million in consultancy payments linked to entities with common directorships, and said the company’s plan to appoint a new auditor made their professional relationship untenable.

The board and its audit committee said they take the issues seriously and have engaged independent professional advisers to conduct a phased review of the fund investments and consultancy payments, requiring management to cooperate fully. The company plans to identify and appoint a new auditor as soon as practicable, subject to shareholder approval at a general meeting, and has pledged to update investors as further developments arise, underscoring continued uncertainty over its financial reporting and governance processes.

More about Ab&B Bio-Tech CO., LTD. JS Class H

Ab&B Bio-Tech CO., LTD. JS is a biotechnology company based in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. The group focuses on bio-tech related operations, and its shares have been suspended from trading following delays in publishing its 2025 annual results, placing it under heightened regulatory and investor scrutiny.

data.title: Suzhou Ribo Advances Lead siRNA Anticoagulant With EMA Phase 2b Filing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6938) ) has provided an announcement.

Suzhou Ribo Life Science has submitted a phase 2b clinical trial application to the European Medicines Agency for its core siRNA drug Vortosiran, aimed at stroke prevention in patients with atrial fibrillation. The therapy is positioned as the world’s first siRNA factor XI inhibitor to reach clinical trials in atrial fibrillation, advancing the company’s strategy in innovative anticoagulation treatments.

Vortosiran is designed to selectively and durably suppress factor XI to block FXI-mediated coagulation and potentially reduce bleeding risk compared with existing anticoagulants, targeting a key unmet need in thromboembolic protection. The candidate is currently the most advanced siRNA-based FXI inhibitor in clinical development, having completed a phase 2a study in coronary artery disease in Europe, although the company cautions that successful development and commercialization are not assured, underscoring ongoing clinical and regulatory risk for investors and partners.

More about Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on RNA interference technologies, developing small interfering RNA (siRNA) therapies for thrombotic and cardiovascular diseases. Its core pipeline targets coagulation pathways, aiming to offer next-generation anticoagulant options with improved safety and efficacy profiles for patients at risk of thromboembolic events.

Average Trading Volume: 362,111

Current Market Cap: HK$12.04B

Fujian Yanjing Huiquan Brewery, a Shanghai-listed subsidiary of Beijing Enterprises Holdings, reported audited 2025 results showing revenue of RMB 640.3 million, down 1.05% year on year, but a 23.14% rise in net profit to RMB 80.1 million as profitability improved. data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited ( (HK:0984) ).

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited plans to bid for the remaining 35% equity interest in its non-wholly owned subsidiary AEON GD, currently held by Teemall Department Stores, through a public tender at a maximum consideration of about RMB170.27 million. The move would turn AEON GD into a wholly owned subsidiary and is classified as a very substantial acquisition under Hong Kong listing rules.

The board argues that participating directly in the tender will prevent delays in the disposal of the stake and allow faster execution of profitability enhancement plans for AEON GD. As the tender documentation cannot be made conditional on prior shareholder approval, the company is seeking an advance mandate at an extraordinary general meeting, while the connected nature of the deal triggers reporting and announcement obligations but is exempt from circular and independent advice requirements.

More about AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited operates retail and department store businesses, with a focus on general merchandise and consumer goods in Hong Kong and mainland China. The company holds a controlling 65% stake in its non-wholly owned subsidiary AEON GD, which is engaged in retail operations alongside minority shareholder Teemall Department Stores holding the remaining 35%.

data.title: KKCG Maritime to Acquire 8.75% Stake in Ferretti After Partial Tender Offer Closes
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ) has provided an update.

KKCG Maritime’s voluntary conditional partial tender offer for up to 15.4% of Ferretti S.p.A.’s share capital has closed, with valid acceptances received for 29,611,598 shares, or about 8.75% of the issuer’s current share capital and roughly 56.8% of the maximum number targeted. Because tenders did not exceed the offer cap, KKCG Maritime will acquire all tendered shares at a revised consideration of €3.90 per share, for a total of about €115.5 million, increasing its stake in the luxury yacht maker without triggering allocation procedures and potentially strengthening its influence over Ferretti’s strategic direction.

The offer has become unconditional in all respects, with all stated conditions now fulfilled according to the offer documentation. Settlement is scheduled for 20 April 2026, when payment will be made and the acquired shares transferred to KKCG Maritime, while final detailed results will be confirmed in a subsequent notice in line with Italian regulatory requirements and a waiver granted from strict Hong Kong Takeovers Code timing rules.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is a yacht manufacturer listed in Hong Kong under stock code 09638.HK and on EXM as YACHT. The company operates in the luxury maritime industry, focusing on the design and production of high‑end recreational vessels for international markets.

data.title: China Youran Dairy Renews Key Supply and Financial Deals With Controlling Shareholder Yili
data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Youran Dairy Group Limited ( (HK:9858) ) has issued an update.

China Youran Dairy Group Limited has renewed a series of framework agreements with its controlling shareholder Yili Group, covering the purchase of dairy products, the sale of raw milk and the provision of financial services from January 1, 2027 to December 31, 2029. The renewed arrangements formalize ongoing operational ties with Yili and Yili Finance Company, ensuring continuity in supply, sales and intra-group financing while triggering connected and discloseable transaction requirements under Hong Kong listing rules, including reporting, annual review, announcements and, for the raw milk agreement, independent shareholder approval.

The dairy products purchase agreement with Yili allows the group to source items such as milk powder and feed-grade milk powder, while the raw milk purchase and sale agreement secures an outlet for the group’s raw milk production. Under the financial services framework, Yili Finance Company will continue to provide deposit, settlement and other financial services, which may support liquidity management but will remain subject to regulatory oversight due to their classification as continuing connected and discloseable transactions.

These renewed contracts underscore the company’s reliance on its relationship with Yili for both operational demand and financial support, potentially enhancing business stability through long-term arrangements. However, the connected nature of the transactions means minority shareholders must rely on listing rules and independent approval processes to safeguard their interests, particularly in relation to pricing, fairness and risk management for the raw milk and financial services transactions.

More about China Youran Dairy Group Limited

China Youran Dairy Group Limited is a dairy producer incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong. The group is closely linked with its controlling shareholder Yili Group, focusing on raw milk supply, dairy product procurement and related financial services within China’s dairy value chain.

data.title: Lufax Overhauls Board With New Independent Directors to Bolster Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lufax Holding Ltd Class A ( (HK:6623) ) has issued an announcement.

Lufax Holding Ltd has announced upcoming changes to its board, with independent non-executive directors Rusheng Yang and David Xianglin Li set to step down when their terms expire on April 13, 2026, as part of a broader effort to strengthen internal controls and corporate governance. Effective April 14, 2026, the company will appoint seasoned accounting and regulatory expert Koon Wing Ernest Ip and experienced executive Siu Hong Cheng as new independent non-executive directors, reshaping the composition of key board committees and signaling a continued focus on governance, compliance, and alignment with Hong Kong listing standards.

Ip brings over four decades of accounting and auditing experience, including 34 years at PwC Hong Kong and senior roles at Fung Group, as well as extensive service on Hong Kong regulatory bodies such as the Stock Exchange’s Listing Committee and the Securities and Futures Commission’s panels. He has signed a three-year service agreement with annual emoluments of RMB500,000, and his concurrent roles at Ping An Digital Bank and OneConnect further reinforce Lufax’s integration with the wider Ping An Insurance network, which may bolster oversight and strategic coordination for shareholders and other stakeholders.

More about Lufax Holding Ltd Class A

Lufax Holding Ltd is a Cayman Islands-incorporated financial services company listed in Hong Kong and on the NYSE under ticker LU. Through its subsidiaries, including Ping An Digital Bank, it operates within the broader Ping An Insurance group ecosystem, focusing on technology-enabled financial services in China and related markets.

China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited ( (HK:0661) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited has announced the current composition of its board of directors, naming Chairman Xiao Shuxin and Chief Executive Officer Zhang Jinzhong as executive directors alongside three independent non-executive directors, Liu Fang, Wang Qihong and Hong Hua. The company has also detailed the membership of its key governance committees, with Liu Fang chairing both the audit and remuneration committees and serving on the nomination committee, while Wang Qihong and Hong Hua sit across all three committees, and Chairman Xiao leading the nomination committee, underscoring an emphasis on independent oversight and structured corporate governance.

These board and committee assignments clarify lines of responsibility for audit, remuneration and nomination matters, which may enhance transparency and accountability for shareholders and other stakeholders. By concentrating committee leadership in the hands of independent non-executive directors, China Daye signals alignment with best-practice governance standards commonly expected of Hong Kong-listed companies in the metals and mining sector.

More about China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited

China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited operates in the non-ferrous metals mining industry, focusing on the extraction and processing of metals critical to industrial and manufacturing supply chains. Listed in Hong Kong, the company’s governance structure is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors.

China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited ( (HK:0661) ) has shared an announcement.

China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited has announced that executive director Ms. Zhang Aijun will retire from the board with effect from 13 April 2026 after reaching the company’s retirement age. The company stressed that Ms. Zhang has no disagreement with the board, noted there are no issues requiring shareholder attention, and expressed gratitude for her contributions, while the board will continue to be led by chairman Xiao Shuxin alongside one other executive and three independent non-executive directors.

More about China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited

China Daye Non-Ferrous Metals Mining Limited is a Hong Kong-listed mining company incorporated in Bermuda, focused on the extraction and processing of non-ferrous metals. The group operates through a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting typical governance structures of regional metals and resources producers.
